Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.S Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JUSTIN S. BLAYLOCK, Esq. of Goldberg Segalla LLP (Tel: 213-415-7200) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF GARLAND WONG's Motion To Compel Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.'s Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One.

Uber defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for Uber defendants (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

There are four separate discovery motions in this matter. There are three motions to compel brought by Plaintiff GARLAND WONG ("WONG") seeking to compel UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ("UBER") to provide further responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One and Request for Production of Documents, Set One. All three motions relate mostly to documents and information which is the subject of the fourth motion by UBER seeking a protective order. Review of the court's tentative ruling on UBER's motion will provide context to the court's rulings on WONG's motions.

WONG's motion to compel UBER's further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, is GRANTED to the extent that UBER is to provide further responses only as to the withheld information alleged to be confidential or otherwise protected. UBER shall serve its verified further responses no later than ten (10) days after notice of entry of the order granting UBER's motion for a protective order.

The request for sanctions is DENIED.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion For Protective Order As To Confidential Information; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Justin S. Blaylock In Support Thereof; (Motions)
Off Calendar Jun-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JUSTIN S. BLAYLOCK, Esq. of Goldberg Segalla LLP (Tel: 213-415-7200) for defendants Uber Technologies, Inc., Rasier, LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., and RASIER-CA, LLC's Motion For Protective Order As To Confidential Information.

Uber defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for Uber defendants (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. and RASIER-CA, LLC (collectively "UBER") seek a protective order to govern the production of information that is confidential, proprietary, or the protected privacy right of Defendant Ghulam Rabini Pazhwakmarvi. Plaintiff GARLAND WONG ("WONG") opposes the motion contending that the proposed protective order stipulation is unreasonable, is subject to abuse, and that the motion was not timely filed.

The CCP does not fix a deadline to file a motion for a protective order other than to state it must be "done promptly." Here, the parties continued to meet-and-confer over the language of a stipulated protective order and UBER's responses to WONG's three sets of written discovery. In relation to these discussions, the parties agreed to extend the deadlines for discovery motions. The court will consider UBER's motion.

UBER is correct that stipulated protective orders are entered into regularly to allow for the flow of discovery while protecting certain rights. They also include procedures to challenge any designation of confidentiality. WONG's counsel points out that the procedure is often abused and argues that Uber's proprietary assertions as to certain responsive discovery may not be applicable. UBER is not contending that it will be withholding any information/documentation they contend is protected, and will produce it once a protective order is in place.

The court has reviewed the lengthy proposed protective order (Exhibit C to the Declaration of Justin S. Blaylock in Support of the Motion). The court finds that this proposed order provides sufficient safeguards and procedures for any party to challenge a confidential designation and will execute same when resubmitted along with the proposed order on this motion.

UBER's motion for a protective order is GRANTED.

Counsel for Uber defendants to prepare and submit a proposed order and proposed protective order to the pro tem judge in Word format at sspaulding@bcsslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Spaulding; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
